facts: A crocodile cannot stick its tongue out.
A shrimp's heart is in its head.
It is physically impossible for pigs to look up into the sky.
The longest one-syllable word in the English language is 'screech.'
A cat has 32 muscles in each ear.
Tigers have striped skin, not just striped fur.
An ostrich's eye is bigger than its brain.
Dolphins name each other using unique whistles.
A blue whale's heart can weigh as much as a car.
Honey never spoils; archaeologists have found pots of honey in ancient Egyptian tombs that are over 3,000 years old and still edible.
Bananas are berries, but strawberries are not.
The Eiffel Tower can be 15 cm taller during the summer due to thermal expansion.
Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood.
A group of flamingos is called a 'flamboyance.'
The shortest war in history lasted just 38 minutes between Britain and Zanzibar.
A bolt of lightning generates temperatures five times hotter than those found at the sun's surface.
Wombat poop is cube-shaped, which prevents it from rolling away.
A single bolt of lightning contains enough energy to toast 100,000 slices of bread.
The average person walks the equivalent of five times around the world in their lifetime.
A rat can fall from a five-story building without injury.
The heart of a blue whale can be detected from two miles away.
Cats have fewer toes on their back paws than on their front paws.
The fingerprints of koalas are so similar to humans that they can taint crime scenes.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus due to its slow rotation.
Humans share 99% of their DNA with chimpanzees, highlighting our close genetic relationship.
Octopuses can change color and texture to blend into their surroundings for camouflage.
A snail can sleep for three years at a time if conditions are not favorable.
Sharks have been around longer than trees, existing for over 400 million years.
Some fish can change gender based on environmental factors or social dynamics within their groups.
Lightning strikes produce ozone, which gives that fresh smell after a storm.
Cows have best friends and get stressed when they are separated from them.
The world's largest desert is Antarctica, classified as a cold desert.
Insects outnumber humans by about 200 million to one.
Humans and giraffes have the same number of neck vertebrae: seven!
A single teaspoon of honey represents the life work of 12 bees.
There are more stars in the universe than grains of sand on all the Earth's beaches combined.
The Great Wall of China is not visible from space without aid; this is a common myth.
Some species of frogs can freeze without dying and thaw out when temperatures rise again.
Butterflies taste with their feet; their taste sensors help them find food sources quickly.
